_note: Status 'mapped' means the component has been mapped to this criterion. It does not mean the criterion has been verified by a formal audit. Run audit-rules.json checks and update validation_status before changing overall_status.

criteria.title: Non-text Content
criteria.evidence: All icon SVGs carry aria-hidden='true'. The close button uses aria-label='Dismiss notification'. Type meaning (neutral/info/destructive/warning/success) is communicated via visible title text, not icon alone.

criteria.title: Info and Relationships
criteria.evidence:
role='alert' on the root communicates the notification role semantically. Title is a

element; body is a

element. Structural relationships are preserved in the DOM.

criteria.title: Use of Color
criteria.evidence: Alert type is communicated by icon shape (help-circle, alert-02, tick-02) and title text in addition to color. Color is not the sole means of conveying meaning.

criteria.title: Contrast (Minimum)
criteria.evidence: Title: #1F2A37 on #FFF = ~14.5:1 (passes 4.5:1). Body: #384250 on #FFF = ~9.3:1 (passes). Color-bg variants use tinted backgrounds requiring per-deployment verification. Action button text: #161616 on #F3F4F6 = ~17:1 (passes).

criteria.title: Non-text Contrast
criteria.evidence: Border on white background (#D2D6DB on #FFF) = ~1.6:1 — below 3:1 threshold. Accent bar provides additional visual differentiation. Requires per-deployment review for color-bg variants.

criteria.title: Keyboard
criteria.evidence: Close button is a native <button> element — keyboard operable by default (Tab to focus, Enter/Space to activate). Action buttons are native <button> elements. No custom keyboard interaction is required.

criteria.title: Focus Order
criteria.evidence: DOM order: icon-wrap (non-interactive), content (non-interactive), close button (interactive), actions (interactive). Focus order matches visual order for LTR. RTL mirrors visual order via dir='rtl'. Logical tab order is preserved.

criteria.title: Focus Visible
criteria.evidence: Close button and action buttons use :focus-visible with outline ring. Suppression of outline is caught by IA-A11Y-004.

criteria.title: Name, Role, Value
criteria.evidence: Role: role='alert' on root. Close button name: aria-label='Dismiss notification'. Action buttons: visible text label. Live region: aria-live='polite' by default. All interactive elements have computable accessible names.

criteria.title: Status Messages
criteria.evidence: root uses role='alert' which is equivalent to aria-live='assertive' implicitly. For non-urgent alerts aria-live='polite' can be added. The component communicates status programmatically without requiring focus.

criteria.title: Component token usage
criteria.evidence: All CSS properties reference --inline-alert-* component-level tokens with global token fallbacks. No hardcoded hex, spacing, or radius values in component selectors.

criteria.title: Semantic HTML structure
criteria.evidence: Root is a div with role='alert'. Close button is a native <button>. Action buttons are native <button> elements. No ARIA overrides of native semantics.

criteria.title: RTL support
criteria.evidence: CSS logical properties (inset-inline-start, inset-inline-end, padding-inline) used throughout. [dir='rtl'] selectors handle content direction changes.

criteria.title: Mobile responsive layout
criteria.evidence: Mobile layout triggered by .inline-alert--mobile modifier and @media (max-width: 768px). Action buttons become full-width at mobile size.

criteria.title: Focus ring visibility
criteria.evidence: Focus rings use :focus-visible on close button and action buttons. outline style defined in component CSS.

variant_compliance_notes.note: Border (#D2D6DB on #FFF) does not meet WCAG 1.4.11 non-text contrast of 3:1. The accent bar and icon provide supplementary visual differentiation. Remediation: use a colored border for destructive type in white background mode (design decision pending).
Tinted backgrounds require per-deployment verification of text contrast ratios against the specific background color values.

Uses help-circle icon per Figma — icon name does not match semantic expectation for destructive/error. Design review pending. Accessibility note: type is still communicated by text, so no WCAG failure.
